summary: Do not ship this design yet: it contains a false upgrade-safety assumption and two validation decisions that can persist materially incorrect usage readings.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: File rename breaks existing launchd installations immediately
findings.body: The claim that older installations keep working until `install.sh` is rerun is incorrect. Existing plists execute the repository path `runcat-poll.py`; updating the checkout to this change removes that path in favor of `runcat_poll.py`. The next launchd invocation therefore fails instead of continuing with the old poller, potentially leaving cards silently stale until the user manually reinstalls.
findings.recommendation: Design an upgrade-compatible transition: retain `runcat-poll.py` as a forwarding entry point for at least one release, or provide an automatic migration that rewrites and reloads existing launchd plists before removing the old path.

findings.title: Legacy Claude scale drift is not contained by clamping
findings.body: The design acknowledges that legacy `utilization` may not share the 0–100 scale, but incorrectly says boundary clamping contains that risk. A fractional value such as `0.75` is valid under the proposed 0–100 check and would be persisted and rendered as 0.75% even if it means 75%. Because this legacy mapper is intended as the fallback when the undocumented primary shape disappears, the failure would systematically underreport usage at exactly the compatibility boundary it is meant to protect.
findings.recommendation: Define and validate the legacy field's scale independently using authoritative or captured response evidence. If the scale cannot be established, reject the legacy reading and preserve the last known-good Reading rather than treating range clamping as schema validation.

findings.title: Discarding an invalid reset can overwrite the only recoverable reset state
findings.body: A successful response with a malformed reset persists the Window without a reset and therefore without any future decay entry. Because the whole Reading replaces persisted state, a transient provider schema error can erase the last valid reset moment. During a subsequent outage, that Window remains at its previously used share indefinitely—even after the real reset—until another successful response repairs it.
findings.recommendation: Specify last-good behavior for partially invalid readings: either reject the affected Window/entire Reading without replacing persisted state, or safely retain the prior reset for the same stable identity when the new reset fails validation. Add a test covering malformed reset followed by an outage past the prior reset.

next_steps: Revise the deployment transition and validation semantics before turning this design into an implementation plan.
Add design-level acceptance scenarios for an existing installed plist, legacy fractional utilization, and malformed reset followed by provider outage.
